ety: for A. مقتضَي muqtaẓā, pass. part. of اقتضَی 'to demand, require,' &c., viii of قضي 'to determine, decide,' &c.
def: part. adj. Demanded, exacted, required, needed, called for; — conformable (to); consistent (with); — s.m. That which is demanded, or required, &c. (by or of); requirement, requisition, demand, exigency, necessity; — that which is conformable (to), or consistent (with); — drift, scope; the ends (of justice, &c.); exact or opportune moment (of time), nick (of time); — adv. Conformably (to), in conformity (with), in accordance (with)
subs: adv. In conformity with, in accordance with; — in consequence of.

def: part. & s.m. See مقتضا muqtaẓā.

ety: for A. مقتضٍ muqtaẓiṉ, act. part. of اقتضَی; see muqtaẓā
def: part. adj. Requiring, demanding, exacting, calling for; leading to; impelling, urging; producing; — s.m. Demander, exacter, &c.
subs: (-kā), To demand, require, exact; to impel, or urge (to).

ety: muktā+Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (dialec., f. -ī), Much, &c. ( = muktā, q.v.). part. (f. -ā), Loosed, let loose, set free, let go; redeemed, released, extricated, liberated, absolved, emancipated (from sin, or from corporeal existence), saved, finally happy; — s.m. A liberated person; — the spirit released from worldly existence and reintegrated with its divine original.

s.f. Loosing, release, deliverance, liberation, setting free; remission; relief; escape; acquittal; freedom; emancipation; absolution; salvation; — final liberation or emancipation, the deliverance of the soul from the body and exemption from further transmigration; the reabsorption of the spirit into the supreme soul of the universe; final beatitude; — liberated state; — unloosing, opening; abandonment (of), giving up; putting off; leaving off
subs: (-se), To obtain deliverance, or salvation, or final beatitude

or mukt-dātā, mukti-dāyak, or mukti-dāyī, adj. & s.m. Freedom-giving; salvation-giving; — emancipator; deliverer; saviour (the term is used by Hindūs as an epithet of the Supreme Being; and by Christians also, as an epithet of Christ)
s.m. The accomplishing emancipation; the attaining to final beatitude
adj. & s.m. Seeking (or the seeker of) liberty, or deliverance, &c.
adv. For the sake of deliverance or salvation.

def: s.m. An opening bud; bud; blossom; — the body; — the soul, or spirit.
part. adj. Slightly opened (as a bud, or the eye); just opening; blossoming; half-closed; blinking, winking.

s.m. A name of Vishṇu (said to signify 'giver of liberation'); a proper name; — name of one of Kuver's treasures; — a kind of precious gem; — quicksilver; — the resin of Boswellia thurifera, gum olibanum.
s.m. A looking-glass, a mirror; — the stick or rod with which a potter's wheel is turned; — the tree Mimusops elengi; — a bud (i.q. mukul or makul); — Arabian jasmine.
s.m. A tiara, diadem, crown; — a crest; a peak, point, head
subs: adj. Adorned with a diadem, or with a crest.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mukkā, q.v. s.m. The fist; — a fist, a blow with the fist; thump, buffet
subs: v.n. The fist to be used; to come to blows
(-ko), To receive a blow with the fist; — to suffer a shock
(-ko), To strike a blow with the fist; to give (one) a buffet.

ety: prob. S. मूलं
def: adv. (dialec.) At last; after all; — in short; — at all, in the least; — but, however (e.g. ham-ne to bahuterā kahā, mul us-ne na mānā).
s.m. Wine; spirituous liquor.
